prompt: The code does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: List<Integer> is not a List<Number> despite Integer extending Number; use List<? extends Number> or List<? super Integer>
addNumbers must accept ArrayList, not List
42 must be cast to Number before calling list.add()
explanation: Generics are invariant in Java: List<Integer> is NOT a List<Number> even though Integer extends Number. If it were, you could add a Double to a List<Integer> via the Number reference. Fix: change the parameter to List<? extends Number> (read-only) or List<? super Integer> (write-only — PECS rule).
